jokes.text: NICE MUFFLER!
jokes.setup: Car with rattling muffler passes by
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic compliment about obviously broken car part
jokes.explanation: Simple sarcasm that establishes Kim's personality - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: OK, ONE THING THAT HELPS... POINT YOUR BOOBS TOWARDS THE ROAD.
jokes.setup: Girls discussing hitchhiking strategy
jokes.punchline: Kim's crude and practical advice about using sexuality to get rides
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Kim's blunt, inappropriate advice delivered matter-of-factly shows her worldliness and lack of filter. Great economy and character specificity.

jokes.text: REDNECK! OOPS...I MEAN... COOL GUY. COME ON!
jokes.setup: Trying to flag down a car for a ride
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insulting potential ride, then awkward attempted recovery
jokes.explanation: Natural awkward moment that feels realistic for teens trying to hitchhike and failing at it.

jokes.text: I KNOW, LINDSAY. IT MEANS, 'HEY, STRANGER, PLEASE LOCK ME IN YOUR CAR, DRIVE ME TO GOD KNOWS WHERE AND MURDER ME.'
jokes.setup: Dad confronting Lindsay about hitchhiking thumb gesture
jokes.punchline: Harold's dramatic interpretation of what thumbs up means to strangers
jokes.explanation: Classic worried dad humor with good escalation and specificity. Harold's paranoid worldview comes through perfectly.

jokes.text: I BET SHE DOESN'T EVEN HAVE PARENTS.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Kim's family situation
jokes.punchline: Harold's assumption that Kim is so wild she must be parentless
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Harold's tendency to jump to extreme conclusions about 'bad' kids.

jokes.text: NO...HE SUCKS.
jokes.setup: Gym class team picking sequence
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of another student's athletic ability during team selection
jokes.explanation: Realistic but cruel moment that captures the brutality of gym class team selection.

jokes.text: IT'S GOIN'. GREAT. YEAH. IT IS GREAT. IT'S NEVER BEEN GREATER.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Nick how things are going
jokes.punchline: Nick's increasingly desperate insistence that everything is great when it clearly isn't
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick moment - his tendency to overcompensate when things are falling apart. The escalation feels very natural for the character.

jokes.text: MAYBE THEY'RE SCARED OF YOU.
jokes.setup: Sam complaining about never getting to play in gym
jokes.punchline: Bill's dry suggestion that the jocks might be intimidated
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that shows Bill's dry sense of humor and loyalty to Sam.

jokes.text: MAYBE I'M UNBELIEVABLY GOOD. BUT ARE THEY EVER GONNA FIND OUT? UH-UH. NO.
jokes.setup: Sam stuck in outfield, never getting a chance
jokes.punchline: His delusional optimism about his hidden athletic prowess
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures teenage self-delusion and the pain of being overlooked

jokes.text: AND WE'LL GET SENT TO TELEPHONE PRISON.
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting in trouble for prank calls
jokes.punchline: Neal's ridiculous concept of a prison specifically for phone crimes
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor that fits Neal's character perfectly - his tendency toward creative but illogical thinking.

jokes.text: I HAD A BEAUTIFUL HOUSE ONCE. OH, YEAH. UP ON MAPLE DRIVE. MMM. THEN KIM'S FATHER LEFT ME WITH 2 KIDS, AND A HUGE MORTGAGE AND, WELL... THAT'S LIFE, HUH?
jokes.setup: Polite dinner conversation about the house
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Kelly's casual revelation of her tragic backstory
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect casualness. Shows Mrs. Kelly's matter-of-fact approach to trauma.

jokes.text: CHIP, MY OLDEST, OH, HE'S A DOLL. HE'S GOT WATER IN HIS BRAIN, BUT, OH, HE'S A SWEET KID.
jokes.setup: Talking about her children over dinner
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning her son's serious medical condition mid-compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the casual mention of hydrocephalus in the middle of maternal pride is perfectly uncomfortable and character-specific.

jokes.text: WELL SHE AIN'T THE SHARPEST CRAYON IN THE BOX.
jokes.setup: Being asked about Kim
jokes.punchline: Mother's blunt assessment of her own daughter's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Common expression but works well for character - shows Mrs. Kelly's harsh honesty about her daughter.

jokes.text: OH, SHE WON'T DO HER HOMEWORK, BUT SHE'S GOT A FREAKIN' NOVEL IN THERE.
jokes.setup: Talking about reading Kim's diary
jokes.punchline: The irony that Kim writes extensively in her diary but won't do schoolwork
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about teenager priorities and the irony of diary vs. homework writing.

jokes.text: DAD, JUST BECAUSE KIM'S MOTHER IS INSANE-- NO, SHE'S NOT INSANE, HONEY, SHE'S PRACTICALLY A SAINT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to defend Kim by calling her mother insane
jokes.punchline: Harold's complete misreading of Mrs. Kelly as saintly
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Harold completely missing how disturbing Mrs. Kelly's dinner conversation was.

jokes.text: AND THAT'S WHAT KIM KELLY IS: A BAD BANANA.
jokes.setup: Harold's fruit bowl metaphor about bad influences
jokes.punchline: Reducing Kim to a 'bad banana' with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold moment - his tendency toward clunky metaphors delivered with complete conviction. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: SPOONING WITH A STRANGER IN THE BACK SEAT OF A VAN, THAT'S A VIOLATION!
jokes.setup: Jean talking about diary privacy being a violation
jokes.punchline: Harold's dramatic escalation to sexual scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Harold's paranoid imagination and tendency to jump to worst-case scenarios.

jokes.text: NO. NO, I LIKE PLAYING BACK-UP RIGHT.
jokes.setup: Coach offering Sam a chance to play shortstop after the prank call
jokes.punchline: Sam now preferring his traditionally terrible position
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Sam getting what he wanted but now not wanting it, showing how comfort zones work.

jokes.text: YEAH. AND, UH, I'M MARRIED TO RAQUEL WELCH. IN MY DREAMS.
jokes.setup: Someone claiming they can play shortstop
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comparison to impossible fantasy
jokes.explanation: Serviceable sarcasm but not particularly original or character-specific.

jokes.text: HE SAID YOU'RE A BAD BANANA. AREN'T THEY QUEER?
jokes.setup: Kim asking what Lindsay's parents said about her
jokes.punchline: Lindsay repeating Harold's ridiculous fruit metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Harold's fruit metaphor, showing Lindsay's embarrassment about her parents.

jokes.text: YOU'RE NOT SMART, YOU DO DRUGS, YOU HAVE SEX... STUPID STUFF LIKE THAT.
jokes.setup: Kim pressing Lindsay for details about what her parents said
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's brutal honesty followed by weak dismissal
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Lindsay's awkward attempt to downplay serious accusations while being completely tactless.

jokes.text: THESE STAMPS... I HEARD THE KIDS PUT LSD ON THE BACK OF THEM.
jokes.setup: Harold and Jean snooping through Lindsay's room
jokes.punchline: Harold's paranoid theory about innocent postage stamps
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold paranoia - turning innocent items into drug paraphernalia in his mind.

jokes.text: AHA! BIRTH CONTROL PILLS. OUR LITTLE DAUGHTER WITH A... SEWING KIT.
jokes.setup: Harold finding what he thinks are birth control pills
jokes.punchline: Jean's deflating revelation that it's actually a sewing kit
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Harold's paranoid assumptions versus innocent reality.

jokes.text: WARNING TO ALL SNOOPS! DO NOT READ BEYOND THIS PAGE. IF YOU READ ON, IT'S BECAUSE YOU HAVE NO LIFE AND NOTHING BETTER TO DO THAN TO PRY INTO MINE.
jokes.setup: Harold and Jean opening Lindsay's diary
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's prescient warning that perfectly describes what they're doing
jokes.explanation: Smart meta humor - Lindsay anticipating exactly what's happening and describing it perfectly.

jokes.text: A DIFFERENT WORD FOR COURAGE. NOT...OH.
jokes.setup: Jean asking what the diary says about Kim
jokes.punchline: Harold's euphemistic way of avoiding saying 'balls'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Harold's discomfort with even mild profanity.

jokes.text: 2 OF THE WORST ONES ARE MOM AND DAD. / THEY ARE THE MOST BORING REPRESSED PEOPLE ON THE FACE OF THE ENTIRE EARTH.
jokes.setup: Parents reading harsh assessment of themselves
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's brutal honesty about her parents
jokes.explanation: Devastating teenage assessment - parents getting exactly what they deserve for snooping

jokes.text: ANYWAY, CAN ROBOTS REALLY BE IN LOVE?
jokes.setup: Diary entry about parents' routine relationship
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's philosophical question about whether her robot-like parents can feel love
jokes.explanation: Thoughtful and funny insight into teenage perspective on adult relationships. Very quotable.

jokes.text: WHO'S SCARED HIS PENIS WILL FALL OFF
jokes.setup: Diary describing Harold as a fascist dictator
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's crude psychological analysis of her father's behavior
jokes.explanation: Shocking and funny insight from a teenager about male psychology. Creates maximum cringe for the parents reading it.

jokes.text: THE WORST WOULD BE GETTING EATEN ALIVE BY WOLVES. FROM THE FEET UP. / UH-UH. NO. IT'D BE WORSE IF YOU WERE, LIKE, A DEEP-SEA DIVER, AND YOU CAME UP TOO QUICK AND YOUR HEAD, LIKE, EXPLODED.
jokes.setup: Discussion of worst ways to die
jokes.punchline: Increasingly graphic and specific death scenarios
jokes.explanation: Typical teenage morbid fascination - dark but not particularly clever

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, DO DRUGS... OH, YEAH, AND HAVE SEX.
jokes.setup: Daniel making excuses to leave the group
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic reference to what Lindsay's parents accused Kim of
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how the accusations have become a running joke in the group.

jokes.text: BEFORE OR AFTER YOU SHAVE?
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber talking about having coffee
jokes.punchline: Sam's innocent question about the doctor's facial hair routine
jokes.explanation: Innocent kid observation that's mildly funny but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: HELLO. THIS IS MAURICE. ANY MESSAGES?
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber explaining an old prank call routine
jokes.punchline: The final call pretending to be Maurice asking for messages
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call structure with good payoff. Shows generational connection through mischief.

jokes.text: WE'D CALL BACK AN HOUR LATER AND GO, 'HELLO. THIS IS MAURICE. ANY MESSAGES?'
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber explaining classic prank call
jokes.punchline: The payoff call pretending to be Maurice
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call structure - well-told by the dad character

jokes.text: FREDRICKS? YOU'RE A TURD. A STINKY, FAT TURD. GO SNIFF A JOCKSTRAP, YOU POOP-HEAD. YOU LOVE PATTING BOYS' BUTTS. BUTT-- YOU BUTT PATTER. YOU'RE A PERV AND A LOSER... AND A STINKY...TURD.
jokes.setup: Bill making his prank call to the gym teacher
jokes.punchline: His increasingly crude and repetitive insults delivered with nervous intensity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - his nervous energy leading to over-the-top crude behavior. The repetition and escalation feel very natural for the character.

jokes.text: OH, THAT SOUNDS TERRIBLE. YEAH. SHE THINKS SHE'S THE QUEEN OF ENGLAND OR SOMETHING.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Lindsay speaking Spanish
jokes.punchline: Kim's resentful characterization of Lindsay as pretentious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kim's insecurity manifesting as resentment toward Lindsay's abilities.

jokes.text: OH! AND YOU SHOULD'VE HEARD HER SPEAKING SPANISH. 'UN MOMENT--UN MOMENT. KIM--KIM!'
jokes.setup: Daniel complaining about Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Mocking her Spanish class moment
jokes.explanation: Daniel's petty mockery of Lindsay's attempt to help Kim

jokes.text: WELL, YEAH. WITH YOU. YEAH, AND I'M A BAD MAN.
jokes.setup: Kim defending herself about having sex
jokes.punchline: Daniel's self-deprecating agreement about being a 'bad man'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Daniel's self-awareness and dry humor about his reputation.

jokes.text: YOU... ARE A SEX-CRAZED DRUG ADDICT. SCREW YOU!
jokes.setup: Kim asking how Daniel sees her
jokes.punchline: Daniel's blunt honesty followed by Kim's predictable outrage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Daniel's tendency to push buttons and Kim's volatile reactions.

jokes.text: YOU... ARE A SEX-CRAZED DRUG ADDICT. / SCREW YOU!
jokes.setup: Kim asking how Daniel sees her
jokes.punchline: Daniel's joking description that hits too close to home
jokes.explanation: Daniel's attempt at humor backfires completely - realistic relationship dynamic

jokes.text: WHAT'D YOU DO, PUT POISON IN A BIRD FEEDER?
jokes.setup: Jean serving Cornish game hens
jokes.punchline: Harold's crude assessment of the small birds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold line - his resistance to fancy food expressed through crude but vivid imagery.

jokes.text: JEAN, YOU KNOW WE CAN AFFORD TO BUY FULLY GROWN CHICKENS.
jokes.setup: Harold examining the small birds
jokes.punchline: His practical concern about portion size
jokes.explanation: Harold's bewilderment at fancy food - perfect character consistency

jokes.text: JEAN, YOU KNOW WE CAN AFFORD TO BUY FULLY GROWN CHICKENS.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the small game hens
jokes.punchline: Harold's practical concern about chicken size economics
jokes.explanation: Great Harold logic - approaching fancy dining from a purely economic perspective.

jokes.text: LINDSAY, WATCH OUT! THAT LITTLE MINIATURE BIRD OF YOURS IS TRYING TO ESCAPE! BUT DON'T WORRY! MY LITTLE CHICKEN WILL HELP HIM 'CAUSE I'M A BRAVE LITTLE CHICKEN!
jokes.setup: Harold playing with his food
jokes.punchline: Full theatrical performance with chicken voices
jokes.explanation: Harold's childish rebellion against fancy dinner - endearing character moment

jokes.text: SMOOTH MOVE, ALEXANDER GRAHAM BELL.
jokes.setup: Coach addressing the prank caller
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reference to the inventor of the telephone
jokes.explanation: Decent historical reference humor that fits the coach's sarcastic personality.

jokes.text: I BLOW MY NOSE IN YOUR GENERAL DIRECTION. THAT'S NOT IN THERE. IT ISN'T?
jokes.setup: Bill reading the prank call transcript
jokes.punchline: Bill adding Monty Python quote then admitting it wasn't actually said
jokes.explanation: Great Bill moment showing his tendency to embellish and reference pop culture, plus his honesty when caught.

jokes.text: OK. A GUY GOES INTO A BAR WITH A PIG WITH A WOODEN LEG.
jokes.setup: Coach asking Bill to tell him a joke
jokes.punchline: Bill starting a classic bar joke setup
jokes.explanation: Classic joke format but works for Bill's nervous response to pressure.

jokes.text: BUT I COULD BE GOOD. YOU KNOW, I MEAN, I KNOW I COULD BE GOOD.
jokes.setup: Bill explaining his frustration about never getting to play
jokes.punchline: His desperate insistence about his potential despite no evidence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bill's mix of self-awareness and delusion about his abilities.

jokes.text: BUT I COULD BE GOOD. YOU KNOW, I MEAN, I KNOW I COULD BE GOOD. / BUT IT'S NOT MY FAULT YOU GET PICKED LAST. / YES, IT IS. I MEAN, YOU'VE GOT ALL THE POWER.
jokes.setup: Sam explaining his frustration with sports
jokes.punchline: Direct challenge to authority figure
jokes.explanation: Sam's breakthrough moment - finally standing up for himself with clear logic

jokes.text: KIM? SHE'S LIKE THE RAWEST NERVE THERE IS. YEAH. SHE'S LIKE A BODY WITHOUT SKIN.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Kim being tough
jokes.punchline: Nick's vivid metaphors for Kim's emotional vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphors that perfectly capture Kim's character - tough exterior but extremely sensitive underneath.

jokes.text: I NEED A POWER HITTER WHO CAN HUSTLE IN THE FIELD, WITH A STRONG ARM. GIMME WEIR. / YES! HA HA HA!
jokes.setup: Sam analyzing player needs professionally
jokes.punchline: Picking his fellow geek with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect role reversal - geeks getting to pick the teams with genuine strategy

jokes.text: OH, MAN, THE GEEKS HAVE INHERITED THE EARTH.
jokes.setup: The geeks getting picked first for baseball teams
jokes.punchline: Biblical reference to the meek inheriting the earth
jokes.explanation: Great biblical reference that perfectly captures the reversal of traditional high school hierarchy.

jokes.text: ON THE ROAD IS ABOUT BEING ON THE ROAD.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking Kim about the theme of On The Road
jokes.punchline: Kim's tautological non-answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim moment - her attempt to fake literary analysis resulting in meaningless circular logic.

jokes.text: ON THE ROAD IS ABOUT BEING ON THE ROAD. / [LAUGHTER] THAT'S GOOD. THAT'S GOOD. YOU...GO ON.
jokes.setup: Kim struggling to analyze the book
jokes.punchline: Circular logic as literary analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim moment - completely missing the point with total confidence

jokes.text: I HATED THE BOOK, ALL RIGHT? I HAVE NO IDEA WHAT IT'S ABOUT, AND THE WRITER WAS CLEARLY ON DRUGS WHEN HE WROTE IT.
jokes.setup: Teacher pushing Kim for more analysis
jokes.punchline: Kim's honest and crude assessment of Kerouac
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Kim's frustration and honesty when she can't fake it anymore.

jokes.text: WELL, I THINK KIM'S RIGHT. KEROUAC WAS HIGH ON BENZEDRINE DURING THE 3 WEEKS HE TOOK TO WRITE ON THE ROAD.
jokes.setup: Kim's crude analysis being dismissed by teacher
jokes.punchline: Lindsay defending Kim with actual literary knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moment where Lindsay validates Kim's instinctive criticism with actual facts, showing loyalty and intelligence.

jokes.text: HEY, MAN, SURE HAD A LOT OF FUN WITH YOUR WIFE LAST NIGHT. MY WHAT?
jokes.setup: Baseball trash talk
jokes.punchline: Sam's innocent confusion about the insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Sam's innocence in the face of crude sports banter.

jokes.text: I JUST ALWAYS WANTED TO CALL A MEETING ON THE MOUND. THIS IS SO COOL.
jokes.setup: Bill calling timeout during the baseball game
jokes.punchline: His admission that he just wanted to experience the ritual
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment - his childlike wonder at experiencing traditional sports rituals for the first time.

jokes.text: THAT'S THE FIRST OUT, YOU MORONS!
jokes.setup: The geeks celebrating what they think is success
jokes.punchline: Reality check that they've only gotten one out
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of the geeks' premature celebration, showing their inexperience with sports.

jokes.text: THAT'S THE FIRST OUT, YOU MORONS! / HUH? OH. WELL... ONLY 8 2/3 MORE INNINGS TO GO.
jokes.setup: Geeks celebrating what they think is victory
jokes.punchline: Reality check followed by optimistic math
jokes.explanation: Classic deflation followed by geek optimism despite overwhelming odds

jokes.text: NO. NORMAL FOOD IS POT ROAST. NORMAL FOOD IS MEAT LOAF. IT IS DEAD ANIMAL, AND IT IS NOT ON FIRE.
jokes.setup: Jean defending her exotic cooking
jokes.punchline: Harold's crude but clear definition of acceptable food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Harold moment - his practical, unglamorous approach to food expressed with perfect bluntness. Very quotable.

jokes.text: WE ARE NOT ROBOTS, AND THINGS DO NOT NEED TO CHANGE. I LIKE HOW THINGS ARE. I LIKE EATING THE SAME THINGS. AND YOU KNOW WHY? BECAUSE THOSE ARE THE THINGS I LIKE.
jokes.setup: Harold defending against Jean's robot accusations
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that proves her point
jokes.explanation: Harold's defense ironically proves Lindsay's diary assessment about robots

jokes.text: YOU LIKE ME LIKE YOU LIKE A POT ROAST? I LOVE POT ROAST!
jokes.setup: Jean hurt by Harold comparing her to pot roast
jokes.punchline: Harold's enthusiastic defense of pot roast as high praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold logic - he genuinely thinks comparing his wife to pot roast is the highest compliment. Sweet and funny character moment.

jokes.text: I THINK OF YOU WHEN I'M... STOCKING FISHING POLES, AND... I THINK OF YOU WHEN I'M ANSWERING QUESTIONS ABOUT CROSS-COUNTRY SKI WAX.
jokes.setup: Harold trying to express his love for Jean
jokes.punchline: His mundane work tasks as expressions of devotion
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Harold moment - his unromantic but sincere way of expressing love through mundane work details.

jokes.text: EWW! OH, GOD. LET'S GET OUTTA HERE.
jokes.setup: Sam and Neal walking in on Harold and Jean being intimate
jokes.punchline: The kids' horrified reaction to their parents' renewed romance
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom moment of kids walking in on parents, but earned through the episode's emotional arc.

jokes.text: OH, MY GOD. LINDSAY... YOUR PARENTS ARE SWINGERS.
jokes.setup: The parents' extended absence and playful behavior
jokes.punchline: Kim's wild misinterpretation of Harold and Jean's reconciliation
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending line - Kim's crude worldview leading to the most innocent explanation being interpreted as the most scandalous. Highly quotable and character-perfect.
